hero.title: Archaeological Survey of India
hero.subtitle: Puri Circle, Odisha
hero.tagline: Preserving the rich cultural heritage and magnificent sandstone monuments of Odisha

home.ticker_1: Welcome to the official portal of Archaeological Survey of India, Puri Circle.
home.ticker_2: Puri Circle manages 40 centrally protected monuments and sites including Konark Sun Temple.
home.ticker_3: Visitor timing for all major monuments: 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
home.intro_p1: The Archaeological Survey of India, under the Ministry of Culture, Government of India, is the premier organization for archaeological research and the protection of the nation's cultural heritage. For the maintenance of ancient monuments, archaeological sites, and remains of national importance, the entire country is divided into 37 Circles and 1 Mini Circle.
home.intro_p2: The Puri Circle was established on November 9, 2022, following the division of the former Bhubaneswar Circle. It oversees 40 centrally protected monuments and sites across 15 districts in Odisha, including the World Heritage Sun Temple at Konark and three Archaeological Site Museums. To ensure the proper preservation of these sites, four Sub-Circles have been established in Cuttack, Dhenkanal, Konark, and Puri.

monuments.title: Centrally Protected Monuments
monuments.subtitle: Explore the 40 historical sites, temples, forts, and ruins under Puri Circle jurisdiction
monuments.search: Search by monument name, district, or era...
monuments.no_results: No monuments found matching your search.

visitor.title: Visitor Information
visitor.subtitle: Plan your visit to the monuments under Puri Circle
visitor.hours_desc: Most monuments are open daily from sunrise to sunset (approx. 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM). Site museums usually close on Fridays.
visitor.ticket_desc_1: World Heritage Monument (Konark Sun Temple): Indians - ₹40, Foreigners - ₹600.
visitor.ticket_desc_3: Entry is free for children under the age of 15 years.
visitor.museums_desc: Puri Circle operates 3 site museums: Konark Museum, Ratnagiri Museum, and Lalitagiri Museum, showcasing priceless antiquities excavated from the respective heritage sites.
visitor.rule_1: Do not deface or touch ancient carvings, sculptures, or structures.
visitor.rule_2: Littering inside the monument premises is strictly prohibited.
visitor.rule_3: Drone photography requires prior written permission from the Director General, ASI.
visitor.rule_4: Follow local guidelines regarding footwear and photography inside active temple shrines.
